payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

tool_args.label: BETTERBUSES
Flatbush Avenue Bus Priority

payload.description: Bus priority project on Flatbush Avenue in Brooklyn, focusing on Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush section in current phase. Three design options being studied: curbside bus lanes, offset bus lanes, and center-running bus lanes.

tool_args.label: Flatbush Avenue, Brooklyn

payload.description: Major corridor in Brooklyn with connections to major job centers, housing, entertainment, culture, shopping, and recreation. Atlantic Terminal provides connections to LIRR trains and 9 subway lines. Intersects with 2/5 trains at Nostrand Junction, B/Q/S trains at Prospect Park.
payload.limits: From Downtown Brooklyn south through Flatbush Avenue corridor

payload.description: Primary route on Flatbush Ave operating local and limited service from Downtown Brooklyn to Kings Plaza/Bergen Beach. Serves over 28,000 average daily riders.
Operates on Flatbush Ave; bus only signal planned at Parkside Ave for B41 and B12

tool_args.label: Bus Only Signals on Flatbush Ave

payload.description: Queue Jump Signals enable buses to get a head start to bypass traffic, often paired with a Leading Pedestrian Interval. 3 Bus Only Signals planned for Summer 2024 installation along Flatbush Ave.
locations: Ocean Ave/Empire Blvd for B41 (northbound and southbound)
Parkside Ave for B41 and B12 (northbound, southbound and eastbound)
Church Ave for B41 (northbound and southbound)

tool_args.label: Transit Signal Priority on Flatbush Ave

payload.description: TSP modifies the traffic signal timing by providing additional green time for buses when they are near an intersection.

tool_args.label: Curbside Bus Lanes features

payload.claim_text: Curbside bus lanes provide dedicated space for buses along the curb, allow for additional traffic capacity, do not allow parking and only permits quick pick up and drop offs during bus lane hours, can be blocked frequently due to parking/loading demand
payload.subject: Curbside Bus Lanes

tool_args.label: Offset Bus Lanes features

payload.claim_text: Offset bus lanes provide dedicated space for buses in the lane next to parking, allow for parking/loading during all hours, buses still pull up to the curb at bus stops, allow buses to pass one another at stops, less likely than curbside lanes to be blocked due to parking/loading demand
payload.subject: Offset Bus Lanes

tool_args.label: Center-Running Bus Lanes features

payload.claim_text: Center-running bus lanes provide dedicated space for buses in the middle of the roadway, include bus boarding islands which physically separate bus lanes while also enhancing bus service, median bus stops increase safety by shortening crossing distances and provide pedestrian refuge, minimize bus-vehicle conflicts as well as bus lane blockages, near level boarding platforms and minimal bus-vehicle conflicts help create high quality transit experience for riders that can resemble train service
payload.subject: Center-Running Bus Lanes
